about.title: About
about.headline: Infrastructure · AI · DevOps · Cloud Engineering
about.tagline: Nearly 30 years building infrastructure that scales — now building AI systems and games too.
about.body:
I've spent nearly **30 years** building technology infrastructure that lets organizations grow, and these days that same drive for building has spilled into AI and game development too.

I spent six years leading IT infrastructure for a private equity-backed HVAC company in Tucson, Arizona, helping take the organization from **$20 million to $98 million** in revenue. I architected and built the entire infrastructure stack from the ground up: zero-trust security on a Fortinet stack, device management for **500+ users**, and the network foundation that let the company keep scaling. I migrated the organization from on-premises Active Directory to **Microsoft Entra ID** and **SharePoint**, moving identity, collaboration, and file services into the cloud so the business could scale without the old on-prem constraints. I put automation in place across onboarding, turning a 30-minute manual scripting task into a repeatable pipeline. I championed AI adoption company-wide, wrote the AI usage policy, piloted Microsoft Copilot, then deployed Claude for Teams integrated with Microsoft 365 and Entra ID, leading a Center of Excellence team that drove adoption across service operations, HR, marketing, and IT leadership.

That same curiosity is what's behind my current projects.

**Sector Lords** is a browser-based cyberpunk crime-war strategy game I built, inspired by the classic 1990s overlord strategy genre (Chaos Overlords vibes) — an original build made with Grok Build and Grok 4.5.

**Corsairs & Corps** is a 3D space simulation MMO-lite I'm building through agentic coding, using Codex, Unity MCP, and Obsidian to manage project state and documentation.

**Hoplon Defense** is an AI gateway webapp: a company AI chat layer with privacy, governance, multi-model routing, and token compression built in. It's part of a larger project helping companies map their operational workflows, surface operational risk, and figure out where AI can be applied intelligently and safely.

I also built **AI Models Made Useful**, a training guide to help people actually learn how to use AI well.

I love learning. Figuring things out and building real solutions is what gets me going, and right now that means going deep with Grok/Grok Build, Claude, ChatGPT/Codex, and Obsidian — whatever tool fits the problem.

contact.body: If you're building something, let's talk.

projects.title: AI Models Made Useful
projects.summary: A guide to get people into AI, use it successfully, and take them from beginner through intermediate to advanced.
projects.body:
## Context

Many people want to use AI effectively but stall at prompts and surface-level tools.

## What I built

**AI Models Made Useful** — a practical path from first contact with AI to intermediate and advanced usage: how to work with models productively, not just try them once.

## Outcome

Live guide hosted on Cloudflare Workers.

projects.title: Sector Lords
projects.summary: A browser-based cyberpunk crime-war strategy game.
projects.body:
## Context

A strategy experience set in a cyberpunk crime-war sandbox — playable in the browser.

## What I built

**Sector Lords** — territory, power, and conflict in a cyberpunk crime-war strategy game designed for the web.

## Outcome

Playable deployment on Cloudflare Pages with source on GitHub.

projects.title: Corsairs & Corps: The Physics of Greed
projects.summary: An ambitious 3D space simulation MMO-lite with greed mechanics, built in Unity (ECS) and DOTS. Currently under development.
projects.body:
## Context

A long-horizon space sim focused on greed as a system — economics, conflict, and player-driven pressure in 3D space.

## What I built

**Corsairs & Corps: The Physics of Greed** — Unity ECS + DOTS architecture for large-scale simulation and multiplayer-lite ambitions.

## Outcome

In active development; public landing / progress presence on Workers.

projects.title: Hoplon Defense
projects.summary: Company AI chat with privacy, governance, multi-model routing, and token compression. Work in progress — public login not open yet.
projects.body:
## Context

Enterprise teams need AI chat that respects org policy, privacy, and cost — not a free-for-all against public models.

## What I built

**Hoplon Defense** — company AI chat with privacy controls, governance, multi-model routing, and gateway token compression.

### Features (v1)

- Streaming multi-model chat
- Conversation history
- Paste images + file upload (images + PDF / TXT / MD / DOCX)
- Org policy allowlists, PII redaction on text, audit metadata
- Token compression on the gateway
- Cross-model context — switch models mid-chat; full history is shared
- Org instructions — firm-wide playbook injected every turn (admin)
- User memory — durable facts/preferences across chats (view / delete)
- Long-thread summary — older turns compressed into a rolling summary

## Outcome

In progress. Marketing / landing presence at [hoplondef.com](https://hoplondef.com); public login is not open yet. Source remains private on GitHub for now.
